name: dockercompose-visualizer
description: Track your docker-compose lifecycle via a dependency tree and status bar.

composeVisual.autoTrackOnComposeUp.description: Automatically start tracking when a docker compose up command is detected in an integrated terminal.

composeVisual.autoDiscoverRunningStack.description: Periodically check docker compose ls for a running stack in this workspace and attach tracking when idle.

composeVisual.discoveryPollIntervalSecon.description: How often (seconds) to poll for a running compose stack when auto-discover is enabled.

composeVisual.composeFile.markdownDescription: Workspace-relative path to the compose file for this project (for example `docker-compose.dev.yml`). Updated automatically when you pick a file in the sidebar; also stored in `.composeVisual/sidebar-settings.json`. Leave empty to detect from the terminal or running stack only.

composeVisual.defaultComposeFilePatterns.markdownDescription: Filenames tried in order when resolving the compose file (terminal cwd, then workspace root). Add variants such as `docker-compose.dev.yml` to prefer them over the default file.

composeVisual.projectName.description: Override the Docker Compose project name (-p) used for tracking and logs when the terminal command does not specify one.

composeVisual.envFiles.markdownDescription: Extra `--env-file` paths (workspace-relative) merged into logs and tracking commands, in addition to files from the sniffed terminal command.

composeVisual.terminalSnifferPatterns.markdownDescription: Optional regex patterns (case-insensitive) matched against terminal lines. When a line matches but is not a standard `compose up`, tracking still starts (compose file is resolved from the running stack or workspace).

composeVisual.healthPollIntervalSeconds.description: How often (seconds) to reconcile container health via docker inspect while tracking.

composeVisual.reconcileOnTerminalClose.description: When the terminal that ran compose up is closed, immediately reconcile container states (mark missing containers as stopped).

enumDescriptions: Count a service as up only after Docker healthcheck reports healthy (or no healthcheck and container is running).
Count a service as up as soon as the container is running, before healthcheck completes.
composeVisual.markHealthyWhen.description: When a service counts as up for the status bar and dependency unlock.

composeVisual.showComposeRunButton.description: Show the Run button in the sidebar header to start docker compose up for the selected compose file.

composeVisual.composeRunExtraArgs.description: Extra arguments after `-f <compose file>` when using the Run button (e.g. `--env-file .env.dev up -d`). The extension adds the compose file path.

composeVisual.showBootTimer.description: Show boot timer on each container card (seconds until healthy after dependencies are ready).

composeVisual.showLogsButton.description: Show log button on each container card. Opens that container's logs in a terminal (docker logs).

composeVisual.showPortButtons.description: Show port link buttons on container cards when the service is up (opens localhost URLs).

composeVisual.showLegend.description: Show the status color legend below the dependency tree.

composeVisual.showDependencyList.description: Show which services each container depends on, on the card.

enumDescriptions: Always two service cards per row.
Always three service cards per row.
Two columns on narrow panels, three on wider panels.
composeVisual.gridColumns.description: Number of service cards per row in the dependency tree.

composeVisual.tierSeparator.description: Show horizontal divider lines between dependency tiers.

enumDescriptions: Show link buttons only when the service is healthy.
Show links when the container is running (before healthcheck completes).
Show links during the healthcheck phase as well.
composeVisual.showServiceLinksWhen.description: Minimum service state required before service link buttons appear.

composeVisual.logsShellReadyDelayMs.description: Extra milliseconds to wait before docker logs when shell integration is unavailable (logs normally start immediately via shell integration). Used as a fallback for compose up and legacy sendText.

composeVisual.reuseLogsTerminal.description: Reuse one integrated terminal per service for logs. When false, each Logs click opens a new terminal.

composeVisual.logsFollow.description: Pass -f to docker logs to stream logs. When false, prints a one-shot snapshot (last 200 lines).

composeVisual.showStatusBarProgress.description: Show the segmented progress bar in the status bar while tracking.

composeVisual.statusBarPriority.description: Priority of the DockerComposeVisualizer status bar item (higher values appear further left).

composeVisual.notifyWhenStackReady.description: Show an information message when all tracked services have reached the configured up state.

composeVisual.serviceLinks.markdownDescription:
Map compose **service names** to URLs shown as buttons when a service reaches the state configured in `composeVisual.showServiceLinksWhen`.

Example:
```json
{
  "frontend": [{ "label": "localhost:3000", "url": "http://localhost:3000" }],
  "backend": [{ "label": "API", "url": "http://localhost:8000" }],
  "nginx": "http://localhost"
}
```

Shorthand: a string or array of strings is allowed (`localhost:3000` becomes `http://localhost:3000`).

composeVisual.openLinksInExternalBrowser.description: Open service link buttons in the system browser. When false, links open in the editor Simple Browser.
